stories.title: CISA Adds Two Known Exploited Vulnerabilities to Catalog
stories.abstract: CISA has updated its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog to include multiple critical flaws across Oracle, SonicWall, Microsoft, and KNX products. These vulnerabilities, including active exploits in Oracle E-Business Suite, are being leveraged by threat actors to compromise enterprise environments.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities are confirmed to be under active exploitation, significantly increasing the risk of unauthorized access or system compromise.
stories.action: Review CISA KEV and apply all relevant vendor security patches immediately.

stories.title: Two SonicWall SMA 1000 Zero-Days Exploited, One Could Enable Admin Commands
stories.abstract: SonicWall has confirmed active exploitation of two critical zero-day vulnerabilities in its SMA 1000 series appliances. These flaws, including a severe SSRF issue, allow unauthenticated remote attackers to execute arbitrary commands on affected systems.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities provide a direct path for remote code execution on edge infrastructure, making them high-value targets for initial access.
stories.action: Apply the latest platform hotfixes for SMA 1000 series appliances immediately.

stories.title: AL26-017 - Critical vulnerabilities impacting Microsoft SharePoint Server – CVE-2026-56164, CVE-2026-55040 and CVE-2026-58644
stories.abstract: Microsoft has disclosed multiple critical vulnerabilities affecting SharePoint Server, including a flaw involving missing authentication for critical functions. These security gaps could allow unauthorized access or exploitation, with at least one vulnerability already identified as being actively exploited in the wild.
stories.why: Unauthenticated access to SharePoint allows attackers to compromise sensitive enterprise data and potentially gain a foothold within the internal network.
stories.action: Apply the latest Microsoft security updates for SharePoint Server immediately.

stories.title: Microsoft Patches a Record 570 Security Flaws
stories.abstract: Microsoft addressed 570 security vulnerabilities in its latest update cycle, a significant increase attributed to the integration of artificial intelligence in vulnerability research. This surge highlights how automated tools are accelerating the discovery of flaws across the company's software ecosystem.
stories.why: The rapid rise in disclosed vulnerabilities suggests a new baseline for patch management volume, potentially overwhelming security teams and increasing the attack surface.
stories.action: Prioritize and deploy the July security updates across all Microsoft environments immediately.

stories.title: ABB Ability Edgenius
stories.abstract: ABB has released patches for multiple vulnerabilities affecting Ability Edgenius and T-MAC Plus systems. These flaws include a critical Linux kernel privilege escalation bug, as well as authorization bypass, cross-site scripting, and unauthorized file access issues in industrial management software.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities allow attackers to gain root access or compromise industrial control systems, posing significant risks to critical manufacturing infrastructure.
stories.action: Update ABB Ability Edgenius to version 3.2.4.1 or later and T-MAC Plus to the latest patched release.

stories.title: Progress confirms ShareFile zero-day flaw behind Storage Zone shutdown
stories.abstract: Progress Software has patched a critical zero-day vulnerability affecting its ShareFile Storage Zones Controller. The company temporarily disabled the service for four days to mitigate the threat before releasing a security update to address the underlying flaw.
stories.why: The vulnerability allowed unauthorized access to sensitive data, necessitating an immediate emergency shutdown of customer storage infrastructure.
stories.action: Apply the latest security updates to your ShareFile Storage Zones Controller immediately.

stories.title: CISA warns admins to patch actively exploited SharePoint flaws
stories.abstract: CISA has issued an urgent advisory regarding three vulnerabilities currently being exploited in the wild against internet-facing on-premises SharePoint Server deployments. Two of these security flaws were previously identified as zero-day exploits.
stories.why: Active exploitation of on-premises infrastructure poses a critical risk of unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or remote code execution within internal networks.
stories.action: Identify and patch all internet-exposed SharePoint Server instances immediately.

stories.title: We built a vulnerability vending machine: AI tokens in, zero-days out
stories.abstract: Security firm Intruder developed an automated system leveraging large language models and code analysis to identify software vulnerabilities. The tool successfully discovered and exploited a zero-day flaw in a WordPress plugin, demonstrating the potential for AI to accelerate exploit development.
stories.why: This highlights the evolving capability of AI to automate complex vulnerability research, likely lowering the barrier for attackers to find and weaponize flaws.

stories.title: Researcher Drops New Windows Zero-Day PoC Hours After Microsoft Patch Tuesday
stories.abstract: A researcher has published a proof-of-concept exploit targeting an elevation of privilege vulnerability in the Windows User Profile Service. The flaw, dubbed LegacyHive, allows unauthorized hive loading within the core system component responsible for managing user environments.
stories.why: Privilege escalation vulnerabilities in core Windows services are frequently weaponized by attackers to gain administrative control over compromised systems.

stories.title: Windows 0-day drops the same day Microsoft releases record number of patches
stories.abstract: Microsoft issued a massive batch of security updates, but a new zero-day vulnerability dubbed HiveLegacy was disclosed simultaneously. This exploit functions as a potent primitive, potentially enabling attackers to perform unauthorized operations within the Windows environment.
stories.why: Zero-day vulnerabilities targeting core Windows primitives pose significant risks for privilege escalation and persistent system compromise.

stories.title: 11 Old Microsoft-Signed Linux UEFI Shims Could Let Attackers Bypass Secure Boot
stories.abstract: Researchers identified eleven legacy UEFI bootloaders signed by Microsoft that remain trusted by modern systems. These vulnerable applications allow attackers to circumvent Secure Boot protections, facilitating the installation of persistent bootkits and unauthorized code execution during the early stages of the startup process.
stories.why: This flaw undermines the hardware-rooted trust chain, allowing attackers to bypass OS-level security controls and maintain persistence that survives disk wipes.
stories.action: Update your UEFI revocation list (DBX) via your operating system's security patches.

stories.title: Critical Vulnerabilities Patched With Fresh Chrome 150, Firefox 152 Updates
stories.abstract: Google and Mozilla have released security updates for Chrome and Firefox to address multiple critical vulnerabilities. While proof-of-concept exploit code is currently available for the Firefox flaws, there are no confirmed reports of active exploitation in the wild.
stories.why: Publicly available exploit code significantly lowers the barrier for attackers to weaponize these vulnerabilities against unpatched systems.
stories.action: Update Chrome and Firefox to the latest versions immediately.

stories.title: Notepad++ security advisory (AV26-703)
stories.abstract: Notepad++ has released version 8.9.7 to resolve security vulnerabilities affecting all previous iterations. Additionally, Ivanti issued patches for Xtraction versions 2026.2 and earlier to address two specific security flaws.
stories.why: Unpatched software remains a primary entry point for attackers to gain unauthorized access or execute arbitrary code within enterprise environments.
stories.action: Update Notepad++ to 8.9.7 and patch Ivanti Xtraction to the latest available version.

stories.title: iCagenda and Balbooa Forms Joomla Flaws Reportedly Exploited as Zero-Days
stories.abstract: CISA has added two critical vulnerabilities affecting iCagenda and Balbooa Joomla extensions to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. Both flaws carry a maximum CVSS score of 10.0 and are currently being leveraged by attackers in active, real-world campaigns.
stories.why: These extensions are being actively exploited as zero-days, posing an immediate risk of full system compromise for affected Joomla installations.
stories.action: Update iCagenda and Balbooa extensions to the latest patched versions immediately.

stories.title: Hack Reveals Suno AI Music Generator Scraped YouTube, Deezer, and Genius
stories.abstract: Leaked source code from a security breach confirms that the AI music platform Suno trained its models by scraping vast quantities of copyrighted audio and lyrics from major streaming services and lyric databases, contradicting the company's previous lack of transparency regarding its training data.
stories.why: This highlights the ongoing legal and ethical risks associated with AI training data provenance and potential intellectual property infringement liabilities for enterprises.

stories.title: Firefox, Chrome, Adobe, and VMware Updates Fix Multiple Critical Security Flaws
stories.abstract: Mozilla patched two critical vulnerabilities in Firefox affecting the WebAssembly and DOM Navigation components. The flaws involve invalid pointer handling and site isolation bypasses, and the company confirmed that functional exploit code is currently available to the public.
stories.why: Publicly available exploit code for critical browser vulnerabilities significantly increases the risk of active exploitation against users.
stories.action: Update Firefox to the latest version immediately.

stories.title: Identity Attacks Overtake Exploits as Top Ransomware Cause
Google Gemini CLI abused as a hacking agent, malware botnet operator
stories.abstract: A threat actor identified as bandcampro is leveraging Google's open-source Gemini CLI tool to automate malicious tasks, including managing a botnet and executing cyberattacks. This highlights the growing risk of AI-powered command-line utilities being repurposed for offensive operations.
stories.why: It demonstrates how legitimate AI development tools can be weaponized to lower the barrier for managing botnets and automating exploitation workflows.

stories.title: Establishing a Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure Program to Work With Security Researchers
Cisco security advisory (AV26-706)
Tenable security advisory (AV26-705)
stories.abstract: Security advisories were released for three major products: Tenable Nessus Agent versions 11.2.0 and earlier, Veeam Software Appliance Updater components prior to 12.3.0.65, and Mozilla Firefox versions before 152.0.6. Each update addresses critical vulnerabilities identified by the respective vendors.
stories.why: These updates resolve critical flaws that could allow unauthorized access or system compromise if left unpatched in enterprise environments.
stories.action: Update Nessus Agent, Veeam Appliance Updater, and Firefox to the latest patched versions immediately.

stories.title: F5 security advisory (AV26-704)
Citrix security advisory (AV26-702)
stories.abstract: Citrix has released security updates for the Secure Access Client and Endpoint Analysis Client on Windows to resolve two vulnerabilities, identified as CVE-2026-53565 and CVE-2026-53566. Users should ensure their software is updated to the latest versions to mitigate potential risks.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities affect critical remote access and endpoint analysis components, potentially allowing unauthorized access or system compromise if left unpatched.
stories.action: Update Citrix Secure Access Client to 26.6.1.20 and Endpoint Analysis Client to 26.5.1.7.
